Bottom line: Civic Hybrid is my best overall pick if you care about driving feel, and clearly the best fun-to-drive pick. It is not the best value pick because Corolla Hybrid and Elantra Hybrid start lower, while Prius wins max-MPG and available-AWD shoppers. Honda’s official pages show the Civic Hybrid at 200 hp with 50/47/49 mpg sedan ratings, and the hatchback gives 24.5 cu ft cargo with 50/45/48 mpg. <citation refs="fiMw4Li24oM8CIht-jXHQ">Honda lists the Civic Sedan Hybrid at “50 city/47 highway” mpg with a “200-horsepower hybrid powertrain.”</citation> <citation refs="Fw3qItp_xlVEsTsSjJ9gq">Honda’s hatchback specs show “50 / 45 / 48” mpg and “Cargo Volume ... 24.5.”</citation>

The key review consensus is that the Civic Hybrid is quick, smooth, efficient, and more engaging than most compact hybrids, but it costs more than Corolla/Elantra and lacks a few premium features like rear vents or ventilated seats. <citation refs="zqTzGlJt-MqSHlwbHQdVw">Cars.com says the Civic Hybrid is “the clear winner on the powertrain front” if you value peppy performance, while noting it is more expensive than close rivals.</citation> <citation refs="zJdbIK6X-SX2tZVq6vfEu">Edmunds calls the hybrid powertrain a “hard-to-beat blend of performance and efficiency.”</citation>

I also checked public safety data.
